name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.grovers_search.grovers_search
summary: Generate Grover's search circuit for a target solution by applying an oracle and amplitude amplification steps.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.grovers_search.build_oracle
summary: Oracle circuit that marks a target solution by flipping the phase of its corresponding basis state.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.grovers_search.amplify
summary: Perform a single iteration of amplitude amplification using the diffusion operator (H · oracle_0 · H).

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.grovers_search.multi_control_z
summary: Multi-controlled Z gate acting as a phase-flip oracle on the all-zeros state.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.grovers_search.multi_control_not
summary: Recursive multi-controlled NOT (generalized Toffoli) gate circuit constructor.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_phase_estimation.quantum_phase_estimation_circuit
summary: Construct a Quantum Phase Estimation circuit with result type attached to the precision register.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_phase_estimation.quantum_phase_estimation
summary: Subroutine implementing Quantum Phase Estimation using Hadamards on the precision register and controlled unitary applications followed by inverse QFT.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_phase_estimation.run_quantum_phase_estimation
summary: Execute a Quantum Phase Estimation circuit on a Braket device and return the quantum task.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_phase_estimation.get_quantum_phase_estimation_results
summary: Post-process QPE task results to extract eigenphase estimates from the measured probability distribution.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_phase_estimation.inverse_qft
summary: Inverse Quantum Fourier Transform subroutine used in post-processing of QPE phase measurements.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_fourier_transform.quantum_fourier_transform_circuit
summary: Construct a Quantum Fourier Transform circuit using Hadamard gates, controlled phase shifts, and SWAP gates.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_fourier_transform.qft
summary: Register a QFT as a named circuit subroutine that can be appended to any circuit.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_fourier_transform.inverse_quantum_fourier_transform_circuit
summary: Construct the inverse Quantum Fourier Transform circuit for converting from frequency to computational basis.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_fourier_transform.iqft
summary: Register the inverse QFT as a named circuit subroutine.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_fourier_transform.run_quantum_fourier_transform
summary: Run a Quantum Fourier Transform circuit on a Braket device and collect measurement results.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_approximate_optimization.qaoa
summary: QAOA circuit template consisting of alternating cost Hamiltonian and driver Hamiltonian (mixer) layers, parameterized by gamma and beta.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_approximate_optimization.cost_layer
summary: Parameterized circuit layer implementing evolution under the cost Hamiltonian for one QAOA round.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_approximate_optimization.driver_layer
summary: Circuit layer implementing evolution under the driver (mixer) Hamiltonian U(Hb; beta) for one QAOA round.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_approximate_optimization.run_qaoa_circuit
summary: Evaluate a QAOA circuit on a Braket device for a given set of variational parameters.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_approximate_optimization.cost_function
summary: Compute the QAOA cost function value and append it to the optimization loss history.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_approximate_optimization.get_cost
summary: Compute the expectation value of the cost Hamiltonian from a QAOA quantum task result.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.shors.shors_algorithm
summary: Shor's integer factoring algorithm: initializes counting and auxiliary registers, applies modular exponentiation, then performs inverse QFT to extract the period.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.shors.run_shors_algorithm
summary: Execute Shor's algorithm on a Braket device for a given integer N and coprime base a.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.shors.get_factors_from_results
summary: Classical post-processing of QPE results from Shor's algorithm to extract integer factors via continued fraction expansion.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.shors.inverse_qft_noswaps
summary: Inverse QFT without final SWAP gates used as a subroutine inside Shor's algorithm.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.shors.modular_exponentiation_amod15
summary: Quantum circuit implementing modular exponentiation a^x mod 15 for Shor's factoring of 15.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bernstein_vazirani.bernstein_vazirani_circuit
summary: Bernstein–Vazirani circuit that recovers a hidden binary string in a single query, using Hadamard gates and a phase oracle.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bernstein_vazirani.run_bernstein_vazirani
summary: Run the Bernstein–Vazirani circuit on a Braket device and return the quantum task result.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bernstein_vazirani.get_bernstein_vazirani_results
summary: Extract the recovered hidden bitstring from the Bernstein–Vazirani measurement probability distribution.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.deutsch_jozsa.constant_oracle
summary: Oracle circuit implementing a constant function (outputs 0 or 1 regardless of input) for the Deutsch–Jozsa algorithm.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.deutsch_jozsa.balanced_oracle
summary: Oracle circuit implementing a balanced function (outputs 0 for half inputs and 1 for the other half) for the Deutsch–Jozsa algorithm.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.deutsch_jozsa.deutsch_jozsa_circuit
summary: Deutsch–Jozsa circuit that determines in a single query whether an oracle function is constant or balanced.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.deutsch_jozsa.deutsch_jozsa
summary: Registered circuit subroutine for the Deutsch–Jozsa algorithm.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.deutsch_jozsa.get_deutsch_jozsa_results
summary: Post-process Deutsch–Jozsa measurement results to classify the oracle as constant or balanced.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.hhl.hhl_circuit
summary: Construct the full HHL (Harrow-Hassidim-Lloyd) circuit for solving the linear system Ax=b using QPE and Hamiltonian simulation.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.hhl.run_hhl
summary: Execute the HHL circuit on a Braket device for a 2x2 Hermitian linear system.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.hhl.get_hhl_results
summary: Post-process HHL circuit results to extract the solution vector x from the ancilla-conditioned state.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.hhl._qpe_for_hhl
summary: Quantum Phase Estimation subroutine used inside HHL to estimate eigenvalues of the Hermitian matrix A.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.hhl._compute_hamiltonian_simulation
summary: Compute the unitary matrix e^{iAt} for Hamiltonian simulation inside the HHL algorithm.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.hhl._inverse_qpe_for_hhl
summary: Inverse QPE subroutine to uncompute the clock register after eigenvalue encoding in HHL.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.hhl._prepare_state_b
summary: Prepare the quantum state |b> on the input qubit register as the right-hand side of the linear system.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.simons.simons_oracle
summary: Oracle circuit for Simon's problem: copies input register and applies XOR with the secret string controlled on the flag bit, encoding the period.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.simons.simons_algorithm
summary: Simon's algorithm circuit: applies Hadamard layers and the period-finding oracle to identify a hidden period s such that f(x) = f(x XOR s).

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.simons.run_simons_algorithm
summary: Execute Simon's algorithm on a Braket device for the given oracle and return measurement results.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.simons.get_simons_algorithm_results
summary: Classical post-processing of Simon's algorithm measurements to recover the hidden period string using linear algebra.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_walk.quantum_walk
summary: Quantum random walk circuit over a cyclic graph using a coin operator and conditional shift implemented via QFT.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_walk.run_quantum_walk
summary: Execute a quantum walk circuit on a Braket device for the given number of nodes and steps.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_walk.qft_conditional_add_1
summary: Conditional increment/decrement circuit that adds +1 or -1 in the QFT basis controlled on a coin qubit.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.build_oracle_circuit
summary: Compose individual phase-flip oracles from grovers_search to mark multiple computational-basis states.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.build_grover_circuit
summary: Build the Grover operator G = D·O combining the phase oracle and diffusion operator.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.controlled_grover_circuit
summary: Controlled Grover operator circuit used as the unitary in QPE for quantum counting.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.inverse_qft_for_counting
summary: Inverse QFT applied to the precision register as the final step of quantum counting.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.quantum_counting_circuit
summary: Full quantum counting circuit combining QPE over the Grover operator to estimate the number of marked states.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.quantum_counting
summary: Execute the quantum counting circuit and return the estimated count of marked states.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.run_quantum_counting
summary: Run the quantum counting circuit on a Braket device and collect the quantum task result.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_counting.get_quantum_counting_results
summary: Post-process quantum counting results to extract the estimated number of marked states from QPE phase measurements.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_circuit_born_machine.QCBM
summary: Quantum Circuit Born Machine: a variational generative model trained to match a target probability distribution using MMD loss.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_circuit_born_machine.qcbm_layers
summary: Parameterized QCBM circuit layers consisting of alternating rotation and entanglement layers.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_circuit_born_machine.mmd_loss
summary: Maximum Mean Discrepancy loss function for training the QCBM to match a target probability distribution.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_circuit_born_machine.rotation_layer
summary: Parameterized single-qubit rotation layer used in the QCBM ansatz.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.quantum_circuit_born_machine.entangler
summary: Entangling layer using CNOT gates between specified qubit pairs for the QCBM circuit ansatz.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bells_inequality.bell_singlet
summary: Prepare a maximally entangled Bell singlet state on two qubits.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bells_inequality.bell_singlet_rotated_basis
summary: Prepare a Bell singlet state measured in a rotated basis parameterized by a local Rx rotation.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bells_inequality.create_bell_inequality_circuits
summary: Create the three circuits needed to test Bell's inequality by measuring correlations in different bases.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bells_inequality.run_bell_inequality
summary: Submit the three Bell inequality test circuits to a Braket device.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.bells_inequality.get_bell_inequality_results
summary: Post-process Bell inequality task results to compute expectation values and check violation of classical bounds.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.chsh_inequality.create_chsh_inequality_circuits
summary: Create circuits for testing the CHSH inequality using entangled qubit pairs in varying measurement bases.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.chsh_inequality.run_chsh_inequality
summary: Submit CHSH inequality circuits to a Braket device and return measurement tasks.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.chsh_inequality.get_chsh_results
summary: Compute the CHSH parameter S from measurement results to verify violation of the classical bound S ≤ 2.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.qc_qmc.qc_qmc
summary: Quantum-assisted Auxiliary-Field Quantum Monte Carlo for ground state energy estimation of molecular systems.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.qc_qmc.q_full_imag_time_evolution
summary: Imaginary time evolution of a single Monte Carlo walker using quantum circuit evaluations.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.qc_qmc.imag_time_propogator_qaee
summary: Imaginary time propagator using quantum-assisted energy evaluations for the QMC walker update.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.qc_qmc.prepare_slater_circuit
summary: Prepare an arbitrary Slater determinant state using Givens rotation circuits as the fermionic trial state.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.qc_qmc.amplitude_real
summary: Construct the vacuum reference circuit for measuring the real part of the overlap amplitude between trial and walker states.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.qc_qmc.amplitude_imag
summary: Construct the vacuum reference circuit for measuring the imaginary part of the overlap amplitude between trial and walker states.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.adaptive_shot_allocation.AdaptiveShotAllocator
summary: Adaptive shot allocator that distributes measurement shots across Hamiltonian terms proportional to variance estimates, minimizing statistical error in VQE energy evaluation.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.adaptive_shot_allocation.term_variance_estimate
summary: Estimate the variance of a single Pauli term from prior measurement results to guide adaptive shot allocation.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.algorithms.adaptive_shot_allocation.terms_covariance_estimate
summary: Estimate covariances between Pauli terms to improve the shot budget allocation for VQE Hamiltonian evaluations.

name: /braket/braket.experimental.auxiliary_functions.random_circuit.random_circuit
summary: Generate a random quantum circuit by sampling gates uniformly for benchmarking and noise characterization.
